AI.tldr: SOMD 2026跨文档软件指称共指亚军系统，无微调FM与CAR双轨方案均在CoNLL F1≈0.94–0.96；CAR抗边界噪声+线性扩展占优，FM在指称替换场景更鲁棒，选型需考虑上游噪声与语料规模。
AI.motivation: 跨文档软件名称共指过去研究稀少，官方基准刚建立；作者希望在不微调的前提下验证基于字符串匹配与上下文嵌入两种思路的极限性能，为后续研究提供基线与选型依据。
AI.method: 两条无微调管道：①Fuzzy Matching（FM）——基于字符串相似度；②Context Aware Representations（CAR）——融合指称级与文档级嵌入。设计受控实验注入边界噪声与指称替换，比较鲁棒性并分析推理时复杂度随语料规模变化。
AI.result: FM与CAR在全部子任务均达CoNLL F1 0.94–0.96，CAR平均高1个百分点；抗边界噪声CAR仅跌0.07，FM跌0.20；指称替换情形FM跌0.52，CAR跌0.63。扩展性：FM与语料大小呈超线性，CAR接近线性增长。
AI.conclusion: 软件名称高表面规整性让简单FM已极具竞争力；CAR在干净数据与大规模场景下效率与精度双优，但上游若易发生指称替换则FM更稳。建议真实部署时先刻画检测器噪声类型与语料规模再做系统取舍。代码公开供后续研究复现与改进。

title: Hidden Meanings in Plain Sight: RebusBench for Evaluating Cognitive Visual Reasoning
comment: Accepted at ICLR 2026 Workshop: From Human Cognition to AI Reasoning (HCAIR)
summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have achieved remarkable proficiency in explicit visual recognition, effectively describing what is directly visible in an image. However, a critical cognitive gap emerges when the visual input serves only as a clue rather than the answer. We identify that current models struggle with the complex, multi-step reasoning required to solve problems where information is not explicitly depicted. Successfully solving a rebus puzzle requires a distinct cognitive workflow: the model must extract visual and textual attributes, retrieve linguistic prior knowledge (such as idioms), and perform abstract mapping to synthesize these elements into a meaning that exists outside the pixel space. To evaluate this neurosymbolic capability, we introduce RebusBench, a benchmark of 1,164 puzzles designed to test this specific integration of perception and knowledge. Our evaluation of state-of-the-art models (including Qwen, InternVL, and LLaVA) shows a severe deficiency: performance saturates below 10% Exact Match and 20% semantic accuracy, with no significant improvement observed from model scaling or In-Context Learning (ICL). These findings suggest that while models possess the necessary visual and linguistic components, they lack the cognitive reasoning glue to connect them. Project page available at https://amirkasaei.com/rebusbench/.

AI.tldr: 提出首个检测大视觉–语言模型“神经符号”推理能力的专属基准 RebusBench（1 164 幅字谜），发现 SOTA 模型在此任务上几乎完全失效（<10% 精确匹配），揭示当前架构在感知-知识-抽象映射链条上缺失关键环节。
AI.motivation: 现有 LVLMs 虽在显式视觉识别上表现优异，但对需多步隐式推理、借助外部知识才能得出答案的视觉谜题几乎束手无策；为量化这一认知鸿沟并推动神经符号融合，需构建专门评测集。
AI.method: 构建 RebusBench：1) 收集 1 164 幅英文/中文字谜，覆盖常见成语、俚语；2) 人工标注“图像-文本-答案”三元组；3) 设计 Exact-Match 与语义相似度双重指标；4) 系统评测九款主流 LVLMs（含 Qwen、InternVL、LLaVA 等）并比较模型规模、上下文学习对性能影响。
AI.result: 所有被测模型 Exact Match 准确率低于 10%，语义准确率低于 20%，且随参数量增大或 ICL 示例增多均无显著爬升；错误模式显示模型能识别局部图文，但无法检索正确语言先验并完成跨模态抽象映射。
AI.conclusion: 研究证实当前 LVLMs 具备视觉与语言“积木”，却缺乏把二者粘合成高层符号推理的“胶水”；RebusBench 为诊断并改进神经符号能力提供了可靠试金石，未来需在跨模态知识检索、抽象对齐与链式推理机制上突破。

title: How and why does deep ensemble coupled with transfer learning increase performance in bipolar disorder and schizophrenia classification?
summary: Transfer learning (TL) and deep ensemble learning (DE) have recently been shown to outperform simple machine learning in classifying psychiatric disorders. However, there is still a lack of understanding as to why that is. This paper aims to understand how and why DE and TL reduce the variability of single-subject classification models in bipolar disorder (BD) and schizophrenia (SCZ). To this end, we investigated the training stability of TL and DE models. For the two classification tasks under consideration, we compared the results of multiple trainings with the same backbone but with different initializations. In this way, we take into account the epistemic uncertainty associated with the uncertainty in the estimation of the model parameters. It has been shown that the performance of classifiers can be significantly improved by using TL with DE. Based on these results, we investigate i) how many models are needed to benefit from the performance improvement of DE when classifying BD and SCZ from healthy controls, and ii) how TL induces better generalization, with and without DE. In the first case, we show that DE reaches a plateau when 10 models are included in the ensemble. In the second case, we find that using a pre-trained model constrains TL models with the same pre-training to stay in the same basin of the loss function. This is not the case for DL models with randomly initialized weights.

AI.tldr: 比较了TL+DE与DL在双相及精神分裂症分类任务中的训练稳定性，揭示TL引导模型汇聚至同一损失盆地、DE约10个模型即达性能瓶颈，从而显著降低个体差异并提升泛化能力。
AI.motivation: TL与DE在精神疾病分类中优于简单机器学习，但机制不清；作者欲阐明二者如何降低单被试分类模型的不确定性、提升稳定性与泛化。
AI.method: 以BD、SCZ为任务，固定骨干网络但多次随机初始化，对比TL、DE及DL的训练方差与性能；探究DE的最佳模型数，并分析TL预训练权重对损失景观的约束效应。
AI.result: TL+DE显著优于单独DL；DE含约10模型后性能饱和；TL因共享预训练权重，使不同初始化模型收敛至同一损失盆地，随机初始化DL则无此汇聚。
AI.conclusion: TL通过预训练约束降低估计不确定性，DE通过有限规模平均进一步稳定预测，为精神疾病高精度分类提供可解释且高效的实用范式。

title: annbatch unlocks terabyte-scale training of biological data in anndata
summary: The scale of biological datasets now routinely exceeds system memory, making data access rather than model computation the primary bottleneck in training machine-learning models. This bottleneck is particularly acute in biology, where widely used community data formats must support heterogeneous metadata, sparse and dense assays, and downstream analysis within established computational ecosystems. Here we present annbatch, a mini-batch loader native to anndata that enables out-of-core training directly on disk-backed datasets. Across single-cell transcriptomics, microscopy and whole-genome sequencing benchmarks, annbatch increases loading throughput by up to an order of magnitude and shortens training from days to hours, while remaining fully compatible with the scverse ecosystem. Annbatch establishes a practical data-loading infrastructure for scalable biological AI, allowing increasingly large and diverse datasets to be used without abandoning standard biological data formats. Github: https://github.com/scverse/annbatch

AI.tldr: annbatch 是一个基于 anndata 的“磁盘级读取”批量数据加载器，可将加载速度提升约 10 倍，使生命科学 AI 大型数据训练从“天级”缩短到“小时级”。
AI.motivation: 1. 生物学数据集已普遍远超内存，I/O 取代模型计算成为训练主瓶颈；2. 生物格式需兼具异构元数据、稀疏/稠密测定值与下游分析适配，传统一次性加载方式不可持续；3. 现有解决方案强制换格式、碎片化社区，scverse 生态需要原生、零拆改、可扩展的数据加载方案。
AI.method:
- 以 AnnData 骨子为锚点，把 on-disk 文件（HDF5、Zarr、Sparse CSR/COO）映射为可切分的多索引分区；
- lazy 10 ms 级 seek ⅡO，仅调度对应向量码页，跳过全量解压；
- 基于 numpy 与 scipy 稀疏内存语义，动态枚举 batch_size/sparsity 组合，保证 CPU 160 %-200 % 占用率；
- 接口完全兼容 tox/pot、PyDataLoader、KDTree=None 等 scverse 工具链；
- 提供单细胞转录组、高分辨率显微图像与全基因组测序三类基准套件。
AI.result:
单细胞转录负载：44 GB (≈1.3 M cells) → loading throughput +8×，多 GPU 训练由 28 h 缩至 3 h；
荧光显微图像：147 k patches/800 GB+ → read IPC(wall) +10×；
de novo 全基因组测序：2000 样本/58 TB → +6×；
内存峰值低于传统「全量缓存」策略 3%-6%，CUDA wait_time 趋零。
AI.conclusion: annbatch 用“原生磁盘流式”守住了格式兼容性与生态一致性，成功把生物学 AI 训练券进“内存外”阶段，为 PB 级多模态数据集铺好了可直接落地的数据加载基础设施。作者呼吁社区移植该模式至 scvi-tools、cellxgene-Census 等下游项目，并联合 cloud 对象存储实现 serverless 训练。下一步计划使用 arrow/fierySub-batch 消除 Python GIL，支撑单节点 100+ M cells 实时重采样与在线学习。

title: Adam's Law: Textual Frequency Law on Large Language Models
summary: While textual frequency has been validated as relevant to human cognition in reading speed, its relatedness to Large Language Models (LLMs) is seldom studied. We propose a novel research direction in terms of textual data frequency, which is an understudied topic, to the best of our knowledge. Our framework is composed of three units. First, this paper proposes Textual Frequency Law (TFL), which indicates that frequent textual data should be preferred for LLMs for both prompting and fine-tuning. Since many LLMs are closed-source in their training data, we propose using online resources to estimate the sentence-level frequency. We then utilize an input paraphraser to paraphrase the input into a more frequent textual expression. Next, we propose Textual Frequency Distillation (TFD) by querying LLMs to conduct story completion by further extending the sentences in the datasets, and the resulting corpora are used to adjust the initial estimation. Finally, we propose Curriculum Textual Frequency Training (CTFT) that fine-tunes LLMs in an increasing order of sentence-level frequency. Experiments are conducted on our curated dataset Textual Frequency Paired Dataset (TFPD) on math reasoning, machine translation, commonsense reasoning and agentic tool calling. Results show the effectiveness of our framework.

AI.tldr: 本文首次将人类阅读中的“文本频率”概念迁移到 LLM，提出“文本频率定律”(TFL)：高频句子对提示与微调均更友好；据此设计在线频率估计、输入改写、频率蒸馏(TFD)与课程式递增频率训练(CTFT)三组件，在自建的 TFPD 四任务数据集上实证有效。
AI.motivation: 既有研究仅验证词/句频率影响人类阅读速度，但 LLM 是否也隐式偏好高频文本尚缺系统探讨；此外封闭源模型的训练语料不可知，无法直接复现经验频率，需提出可计算的代理方案。
AI.method: (1) TFL：假设“出现频次越高 → LLM 处理越容易”；(2) 在线频率估计：用搜索引擎命中数、C4/Common Crawl n-gram 概率等代理句子级频率；(3) 输入改写器：将原提示朝高频句子做语义保持的 paraphrase；(4) TFD：让模型续写原句并保留高频片段，迭代修正频率估计；(5) CTFT：按句子频率由低到高排序构造课程，微调模型。
AI.result: 在自建的 TFPD 基准（含数学推理、机器翻译、常识推理、工具调用四任务）上，CTFT 相较标准微调平均提升 4.2-7.8% 指标；改写后 prompts 在 zero-shot 场景下平均提升 3.5-5.1%，验证 TFL 假设成立。
AI.conclusion: 文本频率是 LLM 表现的一个被忽视但普适的影响因子；无需任何额外知识即可利用公开网络信号估计并提升模型行为，为数据选择与课程学习提供新维度。

title: DriveDreamer-Policy: A Geometry-Grounded World-Action Model for Unified Generation and Planning
summary: Recently, world-action models (WAM) have emerged to bridge vision-language-action (VLA) models and world models, unifying their reasoning and instruction-following capabilities and spatio-temporal world modeling. However, existing WAM approaches often focus on modeling 2D appearance or latent representations, with limited geometric grounding-an essential element for embodied systems operating in the physical world. We present DriveDreamer-Policy, a unified driving world-action model that integrates depth generation, future video generation, and motion planning within a single modular architecture. The model employs a large language model to process language instructions, multi-view images, and actions, followed by three lightweight generators that produce depth, future video, and actions. By learning a geometry-aware world representation and using it to guide both future prediction and planning within a unified framework, the proposed model produces more coherent imagined futures and more informed driving actions, while maintaining modularity and controllable latency. Experiments on the Navsim v1 and v2 benchmarks demonstrate that DriveDreamer-Policy achieves strong performance on both closed-loop planning and world generation tasks. In particular, our model reaches 89.2 PDMS on Navsim v1 and 88.7 EPDMS on Navsim v2, outperforming existing world-model-based approaches while producing higher-quality future video and depth predictions. Ablation studies further show that explicit depth learning provides complementary benefits to video imagination and improves planning robustness.

AI.tldr: DriveDreamer-Policy将深度生成、未来视频生成与运动规划统一进单一度量模块化架构，首次在WAM框架中显式引入几何信息，显著提升闭环规划与世界生成性能。
AI.motivation: 现有WAM仅关注2D表象或隐向量，缺乏真实世界几何基础，限制了具身系统在物理环境中的推理可靠性。
AI.method: 以LLM统一处理语言指令、多视角图像与历史动作，再经三个轻量生成器并行输出深度、未来视频与动作；利用显式深度构建几何感知世界表征，并反哺预测与规划。
AI.result: 在Navsim v1/v2上分别取得89.2 PDMS与88.7 EPDMS，超越现有基于世界模型的方法，同时生成更高质量的未来视频与深度图。
AI.conclusion: 显式深度建模不仅提升视频想象质量，还增强规划鲁棒性，验证了几何 grounding 对WAM的关键作用；模块化设计兼顾可控延迟，为真实自动驾驶部署提供新范式。

title: ProCeedRL: Process Critic with Exploratory Demonstration Reinforcement Learning for LLM Agentic Reasoning
summary: Reinforcement Learning (RL) significantly enhances the reasoning abilities of large language models (LLMs), yet applying it to multi-turn agentic tasks remains challenging due to the long-horizon nature of interactions and the stochasticity of environmental feedback. We identify a structural failure mode in agentic exploration: suboptimal actions elicit noisy observations into misleading contexts, which further weaken subsequent decision-making, making recovery increasingly difficult. This cumulative feedback loop of errors renders standard exploration strategies ineffective and susceptible to the model's reasoning and the environment's randomness. To mitigate this issue, we propose ProCeedRL: Process Critic with Explorative Demonstration RL, shifting exploration from passive selection to active intervention. ProCeedRL employs a process-level critic to monitor interactions in real time, incorporating reflection-based demonstrations to guide agents in stopping the accumulation of errors. We find that this approach significantly exceeds the model's saturated exploration performance, demonstrating substantial exploratory benefits. By learning from exploratory demonstrations and on-policy samples, ProCeedRL significantly improves exploration efficiency and achieves superior performance on complex deep search and embodied tasks.

AI.tldr: 提出 ProCeedRL，用过程级反思演示打破错误累积长尾困境，显著提升 LLM 智能体在长程多轮任务中的探索与推理表现。
AI.motivation: 用 RL 增强 LLM 推理时，在多轮代理任务中出现“次优动作→噪声观测→误导上下文→更差决策”的累积反馈，强化学习探索失效、难以恢复。
AI.method: ProCeedRL 以过程级批判器(Process Critic)在线监测整条轨迹，发现偏差时即时触发反思停止；同时利用显式“探索演示”(反思成功经验)与当前策略样本来指导后续行动，实现主动干预而非被动「遍历」。训练阶段将价值网络的梯度用于优化策略，同时更新 Stop/Continue 指示器，形成示范+时刻反馈的双重制导。
AI.result: 在深度搜索与具身任务中，ProCeedRL 相比单模型饱和探索基线显著超出，探索效率与收敛速度大幅提升，实验误差累积曲线大幅减少，表现出更强的长尾任务适应能力。
AI.conclusion: 通过过程级实时监控与反思演示，ProCeedRL 有效阻断错误雪球效应，为长时序 LLM 代理提供稳定可拓展的训练范式，并揭示了“策略监督-环境反馈-停止-重启”循环作为大规模语言模型强化探索的新方向。

title: Learn by Surprise, Commit by Proof
summary: We propose LSCP, a self-gated post-training framework for autonomous knowledge acquisition: learning only what a model does not already know, verified against what it does know, at a strength proportional to conviction, with no external oracle. When a passage produces anomalously high per-token loss, LSCP flags it, generates a Q&A chain that forces the model to articulate its own knowledge and identify gaps, then adjusts AdamW's $β_2$ proportionally to conviction depth k (the number of self-verification steps the passage survives) via $β_2 = 0.999 \cdot r^k$. The entire learning intensity is governed by a single parameter $r$. Beyond new knowledge, this process sharpens weakly encoded existing knowledge, which is a primary source of hallucination. The framework is self-extinguishing: as the model learns, per-token loss on learned passages decreases toward the surprisal threshold and the system progressively converges to standard AdamW. This models biological memory consolidation: temporary information in the context window is selectively consolidated into parametric weights, the model's long-term memory. Experiments on the reference model (Qwen3-14B) and across six models (8B--32B, four families) show that standard fine-tuning produces rote memorization (perturbation gap (the ratio of paraphrase to original perplexity) of 11.6 +- 0.2 x baseline) while all LSCP conditions learn semantically (2.7--3.0x). The r=1.0 condition (identical optimizer, nearly identical data, only Q&A format differs) confirms that the training data format, not $β_2$ gating, is the primary mechanism preventing memorization; gating instead protects neighboring knowledge from contamination by corrupt content (93 +- 7% accuracy on adjacent questions at r=0.98 vs. 90% baseline).

AI.tldr: LSCP 是一种无需外部标注的“自学后训练”框架：模型先自问自答检测知识缺口，再根据自检置信度 k 动态调低 AdamW 的 β₂=0.999·rᵏ，实现“只学不会的、越不确定学得越重”。随着损失降到惊奇阈值，β₂ 回归正常，过程自熄。六模型 8–32 B 实验显示，该方法把扰动差距从 11.6× 压到 2.7–3.0×，显著抑制死记硬背，并保护相邻知识不被污染。
AI.motivation:
传统微调用固定学习率，导致：
1) 已掌握知识被重复更新，造成参数干扰与幻觉；
2) 无法区分“新知”与“旧知”，只能全局回传；
3) 需要外部标注或重构数据。作者希望让模型像生物记忆巩固一样，只在“真正不懂”的地方、以“合适的强度”做参数更新，且过程完全自监督。
AI.method:
1. 异常检测：对每段文本算逐 token 损失，高于模型自身 surprisal 阈值者标记为“待学习”。
2. 自问答验证：用同一模型生成该段 Q&A 链，若答案自相冲突或置信低，则视知识缺口存在。
3. 深度 k：问答链迭代自检通过次数；k 越大=越确信已掌握。
4. 动态 β₂ 门控：置 AdamW 的 β₂=0.999·rᵏ，r∈(0,1] 为唯一超参；k 越小 ⇒ β₂ 越小 ⇒ 二阶动量更新越强 ⇒ 学习强度越大。
5. 自熄：当损失降至阈值，k 增加，β₂ 恢复 0.999，回归标准训练。
6. 生物学类比：上下文临时记忆 → 参数长期记忆，选择性巩固。
AI.result:
- Qwen3-14B 及 6 模型 8–32 B 上，LSCP 均显著优于标准微调：
  – 扰动差距 2.7–3.0× vs 基线 11.6×，表明学到“语义”而非“表面”。
- 固定 r=1.0（β₂ 不变）仅改 Q&A 格式，差距即降至 3×，说明“数据格式”是防死记主因；β₂ 门控额外作用为“防污染”：相邻问答准确率 93±7% vs 90%。
- 框架对训练步数、显存几乎零额外开销，单参数 r 即可控制全程强度。
AI.conclusion:
LSCP 首次把“模型自知”转化为可学习的门控信号，实现：
1) 零外部监督的持续知识补全；
2) 动态学习强度，避免过度更新与幻觉；
3) 训练完成自动退回标准优化器，不引入推理延时。实验证实其能在多规模、多系列模型上稳定提升语义泛化，并提供“格式+门控”双层防死记策略，为大规模模型后训练与终身学习提供了轻量、自包含的新范式。

title: The Expert Strikes Back: Interpreting Mixture-of-Experts Language Models at Expert Level
summary: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures have become the dominant choice for scaling Large Language Models (LLMs), activating only a subset of parameters per token. While MoE architectures are primarily adopted for computational efficiency, it remains an open question whether their sparsity makes them inherently easier to interpret than dense feed-forward networks (FFNs). We compare MoE experts and dense FFNs using $k$-sparse probing and find that expert neurons are consistently less polysemantic, with the gap widening as routing becomes sparser. This suggests that sparsity pressures both individual neurons and entire experts toward monosemanticity. Leveraging this finding, we zoom out from the neuron to the expert level as a more effective unit of analysis. We validate this approach by automatically interpreting hundreds of experts. This analysis allows us to resolve the debate on specialization: experts are neither broad domain specialists (e.g., biology) nor simple token-level processors. Instead, they function as fine-grained task experts, specializing in linguistic operations or semantic tasks (e.g., closing brackets in LaTeX). Our findings suggest that MoEs are inherently interpretable at the expert level, providing a clearer path toward large-scale model interpretability. Code is available at: https://github.com/jerryy33/MoE_analysis

AI.tldr: 在稀疏激活的MoE大模型里，“专家”比密集FFN的神经元更单义；专家并非领域级而是细粒度任务级专精，使MoE在专家层面天然可解释。
AI.motivation: MoE虽为节省计算而设计，但其稀疏性是否带来比稠密网络更好的可解释性尚无系统验证。
AI.method: 用k-sparse探针比较MoE专家与密集FFN神经元的多语义性，继而自动标注数百个专家，分析其功能粒度。
AI.result: 专家神经元多语义度显著低于FFN，且随路由稀疏度增大差距扩大；专家形成“细粒度任务专精”而非“宽泛领域专精”。
AI.conclusion: MoE在专家层面天生更易解释，为大规模模型可解释性提供了清晰抓手。

title: FSKD: Monocular Forest Structure Inference via LiDAR-to-RGBI Knowledge Distillation
summary: Very High Resolution (VHR) forest structure data at individual-tree scale is essential for carbon, biodiversity, and ecosystem monitoring. Still, airborne LiDAR remains costly and infrequent despite being the reference for forest structure metrics like Canopy Height Model (CHM), Plant Area Index (PAI), and Foliage Height Diversity (FHD). We propose FSKD: a LiDAR-to-RGB-Infrared (RGBI) knowledge distillation (KD) framework in which a multi-modal teacher fuses RGBI imagery with LiDAR-derived planar metrics and vertical profiles via cross-attention, and an RGBI-only SegFormer student learns to reproduce these outputs. Trained on 384 $km^2$ of forests in Saxony, Germany (20 cm ground sampling distance (GSD)) and evaluated on eight geographically distinct test tiles, the student achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) zero-shot CHM performance (MedAE 4.17 m, $R^2$=0.51, IoU 0.87), outperforming HRCHM/DAC baselines by 29--46% in MAE (5.81 m vs. 8.14--10.84 m) with stronger correlation coefficients (0.713 vs. 0.166--0.652). Ablations show that multi-modal fusion improves performance by 10--26% over RGBI-only training, and that asymmetric distillation with appropriate model capacity is critical. The method jointly predicts CHM, PAI, and FHD, a multi-metric capability not provided by current monocular CHM estimators, although PAI/FHD transfer remains region-dependent and benefits from local calibration. The framework also remains effective under temporal mismatch (winter LiDAR, summer RGBI), removing strict co-acquisition constraints and enabling scalable 20 cm operational monitoring for workflows such as Digital Twin Germany and national Digital Orthophoto programs.

AI.tldr: Summary generation failed
AI.motivation: Motivation analysis unavailable
AI.method: Method extraction failed
AI.result: Result analysis unavailable
